t_ch: 唔…光用语言形容可能有点抽象，我尽量给你解释清楚。这尊雕塑是用许多齿轮零件和机械结构拼凑合成的，大概有一人多高，重量…反正单靠一两个人肯定抬不动。
t_en: It's a little hard to describe — sort of... abstract. It's made up of gears and mechanical parts, and it's about the same height and weight as the average person... Basically, it's too heavy for someone to just pick up and run off with.

t_ch: 如你所见，这尊雕塑被摆放在工业展区的入口，因为它象征了贝洛伯格人民几百年来赖以生存、生活和生产的工业精神。
t_en: As you can see, this sculpture was placed at the entrance of the Industrial Hall because it symbolizes the industrial spirit that Belobogians have lived, breathed and produced for centuries.

t_ch: 没有了「齿轮与智慧」，工业展区也就失去了灵魂…我们必须找回雕塑，然后才能把这里向公众开放。
t_en: Without it, the Industrial Hall loses its soul... We have to retrieve the sculpture before we can open to the public.

t_ch: 该去哪找这尊雕像？
t_en: Where should we look?

t_ch: 下层区，看来咱们又得跑一趟了。
t_en: It seems like we'll have to make another visit to the Underworld.

t_ch: 「齿轮与智慧」从最初的设计稿到最终完工，一共花了接近二十年的时间…凝聚了这么多心血的一件艺术品，我们一定得把它找回来。
t_en: It took nearly twenty years from design to completion to make Gears and Wisdom... We can't let all that painstaking work and talent go to waste. We have to get it back.

t_ch: 再设计一个新的呢？
t_en: Maybe we could make a new one?

t_ch: …你知道「齿轮与智慧」从最初的设计稿到最终建成，一共花了多久吗？
t_en: Let me ask you something — do you know how long it took to make Gears and Wisdom from initial design to completion?

t_ch: 二十年！凝聚了这么多心血的一件艺术品，可不能这么轻易就扔下不管了。
t_en: Twenty years! We can't let all that painstaking work and talent go to waste.

t_ch: 我来找你之前刚刚收到线报，有人说在下层区的机械聚落找到了和盗窃团伙相关的线索——不知道这些线索能不能指向「齿轮与智慧」，但我觉得值得一试。
t_en: I received some intel just earlier today. Someone said they found clues related to a gang of thieves in the Underworld's Robot Settlement. I don't know if they'll lead us to the sculpture, but it's worth a try.

t_ch: 等你准备好了，我们就一起去机械聚落吧，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: We can head over to the Robot Settlement as soon as you're ready, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 走吧，去把展品找回来。
t_en: Time to go get that sculpture.

t_ch: 嗯，我们这就出发。
t_en: Let's head out, then.

t_ch: 等等，我还有急事要处理。
t_en: Hold on, I have something urgent to take care of first.

t_ch: 好，你先忙吧。准备好了就来找我。
t_en: Sure, do what you need to. Come back when you're ready.

t_ch: 我们该去机械聚落寻找「齿轮与智慧」的下落了。你准备好了吗，{NICKNAME}？
t_en: We need to search for Gears and Wisdom in the Robot Settlement. Are you ready, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 走吧，去把展品找回来。
t_en: Time to go get that sculpture.

t_ch: 嗯，我们这就出发。
t_en: Let's head out, then.

t_ch: 等等，我还有急事要处理。
t_en: Hold on, I have something urgent to take care of first.

t_ch: 好，你先忙吧。准备好了就来找我。
t_en: Sure, do what you need to. Come back when you're ready.

t_ch: 唔，好多人……
t_en: Hmm, there's quite the crowd here today...

t_ch: 该从哪里找起呢？
t_en: Where do we start?

t_ch: 克拉拉…
t_en: Clara...

t_ch: 克拉拉！
t_en: Clara!
...Huh?

t_ch: …啊，你说那个红衣服的小姑娘？我想起来了。布洛妮娅大人继任仪式的时候，我在上层区见过她。
t_en: ...Ah, you mean the little girl in red? I remember her — I saw her in the Overworld at Lady Bronya's succession ceremony.

t_ch: 虽然不知道能不能对咱们的调查有帮助，但看到熟人总该打个招呼——基本的礼数，对吧？
t_en: I don't know if she can give us any leads, but it's always good to say hi to a friend — basic courtesy, right?

t_ch: …啊，是{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}！还有，这位是…佩拉格娅•谢尔盖耶夫娜姐姐！
t_en: Ah, it's {F#big sister}{M#big brother}! And... Miss Pelageya Sergeyevna!

t_ch: …居然记得我的全名？！这孩子，真不简单……
t_en: You remember my full name!? This kid is really something...

t_ch: 你好呀，克拉拉。你在这里做什么呢？
t_en: Nice to see you, Clara. What brings you to the Robot Settlement?

t_ch: 啊，聚落里最近出了些状况，我正在跟住在附近的叔叔阿姨们打听消息……
t_en: Well, something strange happened in the settlement recently. I'm asking the grownups who live nearby if they know anything about it.

t_ch: …状况？能详细说说吗？
t_en: ...Something strange? Can you share any details?

t_ch: 最近…聚落里有好多小机器人们表现得特别不正常，就像集体出了什么故障一样。
t_en: Well, recently... a lot of the little robots in the settlement started behaving strangely. It's like they malfunctioned suddenly, all at the same time.

t_ch: 出了问题的小机器人会到处乱窜，闯进人们的帐篷里，弄坏大家的财物…很多叔叔阿姨这次都被波及到了，非常头疼。
t_en: They started roaming around, breaking into people's tents, destroying their belongings... It was a real headache for the people living here.

t_ch: 史瓦罗怎么说？
t_en: What did Svarog say?

t_ch: 史瓦罗先生执行了几次集体扫描…但都查不出故障的源头。
t_en: Mr. Svarog scanned the robots for irregularities, but the results all came back normal.

t_ch: 这里怎么一天到晚不太平…
t_en: Never a quiet day in the Underworld...

t_ch: 嗯，克拉拉也没有头绪……
t_en: Yeah, I guess...

t_ch: 史瓦罗先生已经执行了几次集体扫描了…但还是查不出故障的源头。
t_en: Mr. Svarog scanned the robots for irregularities, but the results all came back normal.

t_ch: 所以，克拉拉觉得…或许问题不是出在硬件层面。
t_en: So... I don't think it's a hardware issue.

t_ch: 哦，原来是机器人集体出了故障。唔……
t_en: So they're malfunctioning collectively. Hmm...

t_ch: 不能坐视不理。
t_en: Sounds like they could use our help.

t_ch: 嗯，我同意，既然我们这趟都已经下来了……
t_en: *sigh* You're right. Since we're already here...

t_ch: 好像和调查关系不大。
t_en: This doesn't have anything to do with our investigation.

t_ch: 嗯…听起来确实没什么关联。
t_en: Mm... You're right, it probably doesn't...

t_ch: 但是也不应该坐视不管，毕竟咱们这趟都已经下来了……
t_en: ...but since we're already here, they could use our help.

t_ch: 克拉拉，我们来帮你一起调查失控机器人的事吧。
t_en: Clara, we'll help you investigate what's going on with the robots.

t_ch: …咦？但是，{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}和佩拉姐姐难道没有别的要紧事要忙吗……
t_en: Really? But don't you and Miss Pela have something important to do?

t_ch: 没关系，下层区还有其他人在协助我们调查，不差这一会儿功夫。况且我们本来也没什么头绪，先帮你解决些实际的麻烦，也算是不浪费光阴了。
t_en: It'll be fine — we have other people in the Underworld working on the investigation too. Besides, we don't have any clues yet, so we may as well do something useful.

t_ch: 啊…谢谢你们！克拉拉刚刚还在发愁，该怎么接近发脾气的小机器人…有你们帮忙的话，应该就没有任何问题了！
t_en: Ah... thanks a lot! I was worried about handling the robots on my own... but with your help, it should be easy!

t_ch: 嗯，毕竟铁卫的职责就是帮市民解决问题嘛。咱们该去哪找出了问题的机兵，克拉拉，你能带路吗？
t_en: The duty of the Silvermane Guards is to serve the people. So, where can we find these robots? Clara, can you lead the way?

t_ch: 当、当然可以！请跟我来吧。
t_en: Of course! Just follow me.

t_ch: 看，就是那里！那台小机器人肯定出了什么故障。
t_en: Look, over there! There's definitely something wrong with that robot.

t_ch: 外表看上去倒是没什么不同…走吧，我们上去看看。
t_en: Looks normal enough on the surface... Let's take a closer look.

t_ch: …攻击性真强，这一带的机器人都是这样吗？
t_en: ...So aggressive. Are all the robots in the settlement like this?

t_ch: 不，一定是因为故障才…那个，小机器人，不好意思…你应该是生病了，克拉拉现在要拆开你的外壳，帮你检查一下哦。
t_en: No, it must be malfunctioning... sorry little robot, but it looks like you're sick. I'm going to take off your shell and give you a checkup.

t_ch: 克拉拉拆开了机器人的外壳，进行了一番检查……
t_en: Clara opened up the robot's casing and conducted an inspection...

t_ch: 这是……
t_en: Look...

t_ch: 这个零件…看上去不像小机器人自带的呢。
t_en: This component... doesn't seem like it belongs here.

t_ch: 啊——克拉拉，能给我看看吗？
t_en: *gasp* Clara... can I take a look?

t_ch: 啊…当然可以。
t_en: Oh, of course.

t_ch: 绝对没错…这零件肯定就是从「齿轮与智慧」上拆下来的！
t_en: There's no mistaking it... this component was taken from Gears and Wisdom!

t_ch: 还有这种巧事？
t_en: Wow, what a coincidence.

t_ch: 我也不敢相信自己的眼睛，但我不会看错的。
t_en: I can't believe my own eyes either, but I know that sculpture inside and out.

t_ch: 谁会干这种缺德事？
t_en: Who would do such a depraved thing?

t_ch: 还能有谁？肯定是偷文物的家伙们。
t_en: Who do you think? It's obviously the same people who stole the Meteor Fragment.

t_ch: 原来如此，怪不得铁卫和「地火」花了这么长时间都没找到「齿轮与智慧」……
t_en: No wonder Wildfire and the Silvermane Guards were never able to find Gears and Wisdom...

t_ch: 那么显眼的一尊艺术雕像，不管放在哪肯定都有被发现的风险。所以这些滑头贼另辟蹊径，把它拆解了以后植入到机器人体内保存……
t_en: Keeping such a conspicuous piece of art around wouldn't go unnoticed... the thieves must have disassembled it and hidden the parts inside the robots.

t_ch: 不过百密一疏，他们肯定没想到这些零件会跟机器人自身的系统互斥，造成骚乱。
t_en: But the best-laid plans of snow mice and men often go awry... they didn't realize the parts would interfere with the robots' systems and cause them to run amok.

t_ch: 所以，接下来我们只要找到举止怪异的机器人，把零件从它们身体里一个个取出来就行了。
t_en: Now we just need to find all the malfunctioning robots and retrieve the sculpture pieces one by one.

t_ch: 一石二鸟。
t_en: Two birds with one stone.

t_ch: 没想到还能用这种方式帮到大家…克拉拉很高兴！
t_en: I never imagined this would help you solve the case... That's great!

t_ch: 听着像个体力活…
t_en: Sounds labor intensive...

t_ch: 抱歉啦…不过别泄气，还有我和克拉拉陪你一起呢。
t_en: I'm afraid so. But you'll have Clara and me to keep you company.

t_ch: 克拉拉…会尽力帮助大家的！
t_en: I'll do my best to help, too!

t_ch: 走吧，{NICKNAME}。我们去把被偷走的零件都找回来。
t_en: Let's get to work then, {NICKNAME}. We're going to get all the stolen pieces back.

t_ch: 叔叔阿姨们说，出现故障机器人的地方，大概在这个方位……
t_en: The grownups said that the spot where the malfunctioning robots appeared is in this direction...

t_ch: 一起去看看吧，{NICKNAME}{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}。
t_en: Let's go look over there, {F#big sister}{M#big brother}.

t_ch: 这个区域的小机器人应该都已经恢复正常了……
t_en: The little robots in this area should all be back to normal now...

t_ch: 嗯，零件我们也收集到了一些——虽然不多，但也算是个好开头。
t_en: We also got some parts of the sculpture back. It's not a lot, but it's a start.

t_ch: 嗯嗯，我们去下一个区域看看吧。
t_en: Let's start looking in the next area.

t_ch: 这个区域的故障机器人应该也排查干净了。
t_en: Looks like we've rescued all the robots in this area too.

t_ch: 唔…进展比想象中的还慢啊。这样下去，怕不是得在这里耗上一整天……
t_en: *sigh* ...This is taking longer than I imagined. At this pace, it'll take us the entire day to fix all these robots...

t_ch: 真的有必要把零件都找全吗？
t_en: Is it really necessary to find all the parts?

t_ch: 我觉得可以*适当*地偷下懒…
t_en: Maybe we could just say we didn't find the other pieces?

t_ch: 唔…我明白你的意思，但是……
t_en: Uh... I get what you're saying, but...

t_ch: …好吧，我妥协了！毕竟「齿轮与智慧」的部件数量多得惊人，照这么找下去肯定没完没了……
t_en: ...Alright, alright, I give up! That sculpture has dozens of individual parts — there's no way we'll recover them all at this rate...

t_ch: 我们只要收集一定数量，足够把它重新拼起来就行…咳，缺失的那部分零件和细节，我再找人补上就是了。
t_en: We just need enough parts to put it back together... Ahem, as for the rest, I'll have replacements made.

t_ch: 这事…这事你们必须答应帮我保密哦！克拉拉也是！
t_en: But... you have to promise to keep this a secret. You too, Clara!

t_ch: 好，好的！克拉拉会替佩拉姐姐保守秘密的！
t_en: Don't worry! I'll keep your plan super secret, Miss Pela!

t_ch: 真是乖孩子~那我们去下一片区域排查吧。
t_en: Thanks, Clara⁓ Let's get on to the next area, then.

t_ch: …嘘，小声点，是个大家伙！
t_en: ...Shh, keep your voices down, it's a big one!

t_ch: 小声对机器人恐怕没用……
t_en: Whispering isn't going to stop a robot from detecting us...

t_ch: 呃，也对哦……
t_en: Ugh, I guess that's true...

t_ch: （小声）是个大家伙！
t_en: (Quietly) You can say that again!

t_ch: 可怜的小灰熊…它体内肯定被植入了很多不匹配的零件。
t_en: Poor little grizzly... there must be a lot of sculpture parts jammed inside.

t_ch: 我们来给它做个检查吧，{NICKNAME}{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}。
t_en: Let's go give him a checkup, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 嘿…这下应该就完成啦。大机器人一时半会儿估计还缓不过来，就让它在这里自己恢复一下吧。
t_en: Whew... all finished. This robot will reboot on its own after a while. Let's leave him be.

t_ch: 我取出来了这些零件…给你，佩拉姐姐。
t_en: Miss Pela, here's all the sculpture pieces I found.

t_ch: 谢谢你，克拉拉。我看看……
t_en: Good work, Clara. Let's see here...

t_ch: …唔，数量还是不够呀，而且还差了不少。
t_en: *sigh* ...We still don't have anywhere near enough.

t_ch: 已经…累了……
t_en: I'm... already exhausted...

t_ch: 不瞒你说，我也觉得有些乏了。
t_en: To be honest, I'm a little worn out myself.

t_ch: 还要继续锤机器人吗？我奉陪。
t_en: Want to keep on fixing robots? I'm ready to help.

t_ch: 也只能继续了吧？其实我已经有点乏了……
t_en: Do we have any other choice? I am starting to feel a little worn out, though...

t_ch: 那个…要不，我们还是去问问史瓦罗先生吧？
t_en: I know, how about we go talk to Mr. Svarog?

t_ch: 虽然他没办法把出了故障的小机器人全都召集在一起，但说不定史瓦罗先生能帮我们出出主意……
t_en: He might not be able to round up the robots, but I'm sure he'll have some good ideas.

t_ch: 啊…史瓦罗，我还没见过他呢。听说他不太好相处……
t_en: Umm... I've never met Svarog before. I heard he's not easy to get along with...

t_ch: 不会不会，史瓦罗先生现在可好客啦。跟克拉拉来吧，我会和他说清楚情况的。
t_en: Don't worry, Mr. Svarog has warmed up to guests a lot more now. I'll explain the whole situation to him. Here, this way.

t_ch: 咦？史瓦罗先生…在和小朋友聊天？
t_en: Huh? Is Mr. Svarog... talking to other children?

t_ch: 好少见…发生什么了吗？
t_en: That's not usually like him. I wonder what's going on?

t_ch: …克拉拉，你回来了。还带了{NICKNAME}，和……
t_en: Clara, you have returned. And you have brought with you the outsider, {NICKNAME}, and...

t_ch: 检索资料中…佩拉格娅•谢尔盖耶夫娜，银鬃铁卫情报官。幸会。
t_en: Querying database... Silvermane Guard Intelligence Officer Pelageya Sergeyevna. It is a pleasure to meet you.

t_ch: …初次见面，你好，史瓦罗先生。
t_en: ...It's nice to meet you, Mr. Svarog.

t_ch: 啊，克拉拉姐姐回来啦！
t_en: Hey, big sis Clara is back!

t_ch: 克拉拉姐姐……
t_en: Big sis Clara...

t_ch: 你们好呀。你们两个来找史瓦罗先生，是有什么事吗？
t_en: Hello! What brings you two here today?

t_ch: 这两个人类幼崽找到了一批可疑的零件，想把它们交付给我，以换取「赞赏」。但这些零件来源不明，且不具备实用价值。仅凭计算难以断定……
t_en: These two young humans found a suspicious batch of components. They wished to deliver them to me and earn my "appreciation." However, the origin of these components is unknown and they have no practical utility. It is difficult to ascertain their value...

t_ch: …没用的零件？能给我们看看吗？
t_en: ...Suspicious components? Can we have a look at them?

t_ch: 当然。你自便吧。
t_en: As you wish.

t_ch: 和佩拉一起检查了孩子们收集到的零件……
t_en: You and Pela check out the parts collected by the children.

t_ch: 果然！这些也是从「齿轮与智慧」上拆卸下来的部件！
t_en: Sure enough! These parts are from the museum's sculpture!

t_ch: 你们从哪里弄来的？
t_en: Where did you guys find the parts?

t_ch: 数量比我们之前找到的多好多啊…这么一想，感觉有点泄气……
t_en: These kids found way more than we did fixing all those robots... what an embarrassment...

t_ch: 数量比我们找到的多得多…
t_en: They found way more parts than we did...

t_ch: 的确。这么一想，感觉还有点泄气……
t_en: Yep, we were outdone by a couple of kids. Kind of embarrassing when you think about it...

t_ch: 我和妹妹在聚落附近玩的时候，正好听到有几个大人在商量该怎么处理这些零件。
t_en: Me and my sister were playing outside — we heard some grownups talking about what they were going to do with the parts.

t_ch: 他们讨论的内容很不对劲！反正就是…鬼鬼祟祟的，净是些见不得人的话！
t_en: It seemed suspicious... They were acting all sneaky!

t_ch: 我和妹妹在旁边听了很久，一直等到他们几个「呼呼呼」地睡着了……
t_en: We hid in the shadows and waited until we heard them snoring...

t_ch: 然后，然后我们就悄悄地把装零件的箱子拖到了这里，想把它们还给史瓦罗先生……
t_en: And then, and then — we quietly dragged away the boxes with the parts in, a—and gave them to Mr. Svarog...

t_ch: 你们这么做也太冒险了！万一惊醒了那些家伙，情况可就危险了。
t_en: That was really risky behavior! If those grownups had woken up, things could have gotten really dangerous for you two.

t_ch: 呜…我和哥哥，我们只是想帮上忙……
t_en: *whimper* W—We were just trying to help...

t_ch: …哎呀，别哭别哭！听我说，虽然这些零件不是史瓦罗先生丢的…但你们还是帮上了很大的忙哦！
t_en: ...Oh, don't cry! Uh — listen, these parts don't belong to Mr. Svarog, but... you two were still a big help to us!

t_ch: 快夸她，史瓦罗！
t_en: Quick, Svarog, say something nice!

t_ch: 我尚未理清事态的脉络，但如果这是{NICKNAME}的诉求——
t_en: I am unable to determine the logic behind this series of events, but if you insist...

t_ch: ——做得好，小姑娘。
t_en: ...excellent job, little girl.

t_ch: 没错，真是救命了。
t_en: Yes, you two are real lifesavers!

t_ch: 我尚未理清事态的脉络，但如果你们的行为帮助到了{NICKNAME}和{F#她}{M#他}的同伴——
t_en: I am unable to determine the logic behind your actions, but if you managed to help {NICKNAME} and {F#her}{M#his} companions...

t_ch: ——那就值得夸赞，小姑娘。
t_en: ...then you are indeed worthy of praise, little girl.

t_ch: 史瓦罗先生…变得更温柔了呢。
t_en: See, I told you Mr. Svarog had warmed up to guests.

t_ch: 真的吗？我们确实有帮上忙，对吧？太好啦！
t_en: Really? So we were a big help, weren't we? Awesome!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，一下收集到了这么多部件，至少应该足够还原「齿轮与智慧」的大致结构了吧？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, with these parts, we should have enough to put the sculpture back together... more or less.

t_ch: 那两个孩子说的话很让人在意…看来这个聚落里也有参与了展品盗窃案的家伙在活动。
t_en: This isn't good... If what those kids said is true, then the museum thieves have operatives working in the settlement.

t_ch: 我马上联系铁卫和「地火」的人，让他们来这里彻查。不过现在…咱们还是先赶紧把这些零件带回博物馆吧，免得夜长梦多。
t_en: I'll get in touch with Wildfire and the Silvermane Guards right away and have them investigate the matter thoroughly. But for now... let's take the parts we have back to the museum, or we'll be here all night.

t_ch: 欢迎，{NICKNAME}——你为何而来？
t_en: Welcome, {NICKNAME}. What brings you here?

t_ch: 你和人类相处得越来越好了。
t_en: You've gotten better at getting along with humans.

t_ch: 这是你经过观察后得出的结论吗？
t_en: Did you arrive at such a conclusion through direct observation?

t_ch: 我的各项情感读数近期并无波动。但克拉拉也对我说过类似的话。
t_en: Clara too has conveyed similar sentiments, though my emotional indices have not experienced any fluctuations.

t_ch: 如果你们一致认定如此，那一定是某种凌驾于算法范畴之上的力量影响了我的系统行为。
t_en: If you and Clara have both reached the same conclusion independently, then there must be some force influencing my behavior which transcends my system's algorithms.

t_ch: 这感觉也许怪异…但并不坏。
t_en: This possibility strikes me as peculiar... but not necessarily bad.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}，还有佩拉姐姐，这次能帮到你们的忙——虽然只是巧合——克拉拉很开心。
t_en: I'm really happy that I got to help you and Miss Pela today, {F#big sister}{M#big brother}. Even if it was by accident.

t_ch: 最近，在聚落的大家都会更频繁地露出笑容了…克拉拉觉得很幸福。
t_en: People in the settlement have been smiling a lot more these days... and that makes me happy too.

t_ch: 克拉拉希望能和史瓦罗先生一起守护这片平静…有空的话，最好能带他去上层区看看。
t_en: I hope me and Mr. Svarog can make sure it stays that way... And if we ever get the chance, I want to show Mr. Svarog around the Overworld.

t_ch: 杰姬，你听见了吗？史瓦罗大佬表扬你了！
t_en: Did you hear that, Jackie? Mr. Svarog said nice things about you!

t_ch: 他、他也表扬了哥哥，是我们两个人……
t_en: H—He said nice things about you too...

t_ch: 听好，以后我们也要坚持做好事，争取成为史瓦罗大佬最信任…啊，是除了克拉拉姐姐之外最信任的人！
t_en: Look, from now on we have to do good things only — that way we can become Mr. Svarog's most trustworthy... uh — second most trustworthy friends, after Clara of course.

t_ch: 杰、杰姬会一直努力的……
t_en: O—Okay, I'll do my best...

t_ch: 重组「齿轮与智慧」
t_en: Rebuild Gears and Wisdom

t_ch: 和佩拉一起努力拼凑好了「齿轮与智慧」……
t_en: You and Pela work strenuously to reassemble Gears and Wisdom...

t_ch: 呼…呼…真是费了好大的力气，早知道就再多叫几个帮手了……
t_en: *sigh* If I'd known how much work that was going to be, I would've enlisted more help...

t_ch: 但是你看——最后的成品还是挺像模像样的嘛！
t_en: But see the final product for yourself — not too shabby, is it?

t_ch: 还原度有多高？
t_en: How different is it from the original?

t_ch: 唔…如果不拿着设计蓝图、花上十几个小时仔细做对比的话…应该完全看不出区别吧？对，差不多还原到了这个程度——干得漂亮，{NICKNAME}！
t_en: Well... as long as you don't pull out the blueprints and spend dozens of hours comparing... this version is virtually indistinguishable from the original. Excellent work, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 拆了再装一遍吧，我还没过瘾。
t_en: Putting it back together was really fun. Let's tear it down and do it again!

t_ch: …想想可以，如果真要上手的话，我就要叫铁卫来把你带走了！
t_en: ...You wanna know what happens to people who vandalize exhibits? I call the Silvermane Guards and they carry you away!

t_ch: 不过你看，这雕塑——如果不拿着设计蓝图细细比对上十几个小时，应该完全看不出和原件的区别吧？干得漂亮，{NICKNAME}！
t_en: Well... as long as you don't pull out the blueprints and spend dozens of hours comparing... this version is virtually indistinguishable from the original. Excellent work, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 没想到居然要亲手还原…
t_en: I never thought we'd be rebuilding it ourselves...

t_ch: 唔…毕竟咱们没有收集全每一个零件嘛，要是叫专家来复原的话岂不就露馅了……
t_en: Well... Technically we didn't find all the parts, and if we called in the experts to put it back together, they would have realized...

t_ch: 不过你看，这雕塑——如果不拿着设计蓝图细细比对上十几个小时，应该完全看不出和原件的区别吧？干得漂亮，{NICKNAME}！
t_en: ...but take a look — as long as you don't pull out the blueprints and spend a dozen hours comparing... this version is virtually indistinguishable from the original. Excellent work, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 找回了「齿轮与智慧」这个门面，<color=#dbc291ff>工业展馆也就可以对公众开放了。</color>
t_en: With Gears and Wisdom back, <color=#dbc291ff>the Industrial Hall can reopen to the public</color>.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，要是你还对博物馆的经营感兴趣的话…不妨再去和助理艾丽斯小姐说一声，聊聊工业展区开放的相关事宜？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, if you're still interested in managing the museum... why don't you go talk to Miss Eris about opening up the Industrial Hall?

t_ch: 我要接着去调查展品失窃案了，不知道其他人有没有在史瓦罗的地盘上找到什么决定性的线索……
t_en: In the meantime, I'll follow up on the exhibit theft case. I wonder if any new clues were found over in Svarog's domain...

t_ch: 一有消息我马上通知你，{NICKNAME}。我有很强烈的预感，我们就快揪出幕后黑手了。
t_en: I'll let you know if we get any new leads, {NICKNAME}. I have a hunch that we're close to uncovering the culprit behind the curtain.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，又见面了！我都听艾丽斯小姐说了，在你的妥善经营之下，博物馆已经快要恢复鼎盛期间的兴旺人流了。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, good to see you again! I've heard from Miss Eris that under your superb management, the museum is on the verge of regaining the thriving crowds of its heyday.

t_ch: 照现在的势头继续下去，只要历史展区也能顺利恢复开放…博物馆完全复兴的一天，指日可待！
t_en: If the momentum continues, and if the History-Culture Hall can reopen on schedule... the museum's complete revival is surely just around the corner!

t_ch: 这次又丢了什么？
t_en: What went missing this time?

t_ch: 看来你已经抓住规律了嘛……
t_en: ...So you guessed it already.

t_ch: 这都多亏了我。
t_en: Good thing you have me around.

t_ch: …咳，可不是嘛。
t_en: ...Ahem. Yes, indeed.

t_ch: 这次的情况和之前类似——贝洛伯格博物馆的最后一个展区，历史馆，有一件重要的展品失窃了。说起来，这件展品和你还有些关系。
t_en: ...I'm afraid there's been another theft. Like the previous cases, a valuable item was stolen from our exhibit — this time in the History-Culture Hall. The item in question has a connection to you.

t_ch: 我的照片？现在就给你拍一张。
t_en: Was it a photo of me? I can always take a new one for you.

t_ch: …不是啦，那种东西随时都能补拍，你以为我不知道吗？
t_en: No. As you just pointed out, that kind of thing is easily replaced.

t_ch: 我的武器？送你了。
t_en: Was it my weapon? Here — I'll give it to you right now.

t_ch: 呃，这倒不必，我们其实已经做了高仿模型……
t_en: Uh, that won't be necessary — we've already made a high-quality replica.

t_ch: 是什么呢？
t_en: So what is it?

t_ch: 嘿嘿，感兴趣吧？
t_en: Hehe, have I piqued your curiosity?

t_ch: 唔，在这里站着总觉得解释不清，要不我带你去历史区亲眼看看吧。
t_en: It'll be easier to explain once we're actually in the History-Culture Hall. Why don't I take you there now?

t_ch: 有了这样东西，所有贝洛伯格人就都能了解到你们的故事了，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: With an exhibit like this, all Belobogians will know your story, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 历史区，顾名思义，保存着对于这座城市而言拥有重要历史意义的文物、复制品、还有各类文献。
t_en: You can probably guess from the name that the History-Culture Hall houses artifacts, replicas, and documents that are of great historical significance to Belobog.

t_ch: 只有铭记前人的努力和教训，我们才能在日渐艰难的环境中存续下来，总结出属于每一代贝洛伯格人的生存之路。
t_en: Every passing day, we Belobogians must continue to endure the city's harsh environment. The trials and experiences of our ancestors serve as a reminder that the path of survival belongs to every generation of our people.

t_ch: 贝洛伯格的每个孩子都至少应该来这里一次…不，不对，一次可远远不够。
t_en: Every child in Belobog should visit this exhibit at least once... No, once isn't nearly enough.

t_ch: 就是它啦。
t_en: This is the item in question.

t_ch: 这台投影仪是专为博物馆定制的限定款，全贝洛伯格仅此一台，它成像的清晰度……
t_en: This projector is a limited-edition version custom-made for the museum — the only one of its kind in the whole of Belobog. It can produce images with a resolution of up to...

t_ch: 咳，不好意思，又犯了当讲解员的老毛病了…你见多识广，肯定见过很多比它更精密的设备。
t_en: ...Oh, sorry, I'm doing my tour guide thing again. I'm sure someone as well-traveled as yourself has seen much fancier equipment than this.

t_ch: 要修东西可以去找希露瓦嘛。
t_en: So broken, not stolen? Let's ask Serval.

t_ch: 啊，不，你误会啦。
t_en: Oh, uh, that's not the issue...

t_ch: 哇，高科技！
t_en: Wowee! This is so high tech!

t_ch: …下次记得做好表情管理。
t_en: ...Nice try keeping a straight face.

t_ch: 投影仪本身倒是没什么问题，但作为展出内容最为关键的那卷<color=#dbc291ff>「微缩胶片」</color>却在此前失窃了。
t_en: The issue isn't the projector, but the <color=#dbc291ff>microfilm</color> that goes into it. It's the most crucial piece of this exhibit, and it was stolen.

t_ch: 那卷胶片上有一些十分珍贵的图像素材，之前我们还没来得及复制原片…不得不说，那些窃贼下手又快又准，而且很有辨识展品价值的眼光。
t_en: There were some very valuable images on that film, and we hadn't gotten around to making a backup... I must admit, the thieves chose a good target, and they were fast and efficient.

t_ch: 幻灯片投影是这次博物馆翻新后才会推出的新项目，同时也是我们为前来观展的市民准备的惊喜——要是找不到原片，历史区的开放就只能无限期地后延了。
t_en: Slide projection is a new addition to the museum — it was intended to be a surprise for visitors when the History-Culture Hall reopened. If we don't recover the microfilm, the Hall's opening will have to be postponed indefinitely.

t_ch: 而且「微缩胶片」里还存着跟你、三月小姐和丹恒先生相关的图像，策展方想借此机会，把星穹列车到访贝洛伯格的故事讲给所有人听。
t_en: Some of the images stored on that film are related to you, Miss March 7th, and Master Dan Heng. The curators of the museum wanted to take this opportunity to tell the story of the Astral Express' visit to Belobog.

t_ch: 什么时候偷拍的？！
t_en: You did a secret photoshoot of us?

t_ch: 咳…这、这不是重点。
t_en: Uhh... th—that's beside the point.

t_ch: 我们可以补拍——
t_en: No worries. We can just do a reshoot.

t_ch: 和你们有关的内容只是其中一部分啦，还有很多其它要展示的图像……
t_en: The parts related to you are only a small portion of the collection...

t_ch: …{NICKNAME}，你身上是不是有什么东西在响？
t_en: ...{NICKNAME}, is... that your phone?

t_ch: 是布洛妮娅大人的消息吗？看来她也觉得这件事已经拖得太久，必须亲自介入了……
t_en: Was that a message from Lady Bronya? Hm, this theft case has been dragging on for too long — she must have decided to intervene...

t_ch: 如果她能调用更多的资源参与调查，案件应该很快就能有所进展。
t_en: If she can delegate more resources to the investigation, that should help it move forward faster.

t_ch: 事不宜迟，我们现在就去克里珀堡找她吧，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: No point in waiting around here, then. Let's go pay Lady Bronya a visit, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，佩拉，你们好。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, Pela, welcome.

t_ch: 我没猜错的话，你们是一起从历史文化博物馆赶过来的？这段时间里真是辛苦二位了。
t_en: If I'm not mistaken, the two of you just rushed over here from the History and Culture Museum? I appreciate your coming here on such short notice.

t_ch: 我本该早些介入这起事件，但之前一直有其它要务缠身，没能及时响应——给你们徒增了很多工作量，实在抱歉。
t_en: I should have gotten involved earlier, but I was tied up with other responsibilities. My apologies for letting you take on the investigation without proper support.

t_ch: 布、布洛妮娅大人，您不必如此……
t_en: Lady Bronya, we never saw it like that...

t_ch: …嘿，这里只有我们几个，你不必这么拘礼，佩拉。
t_en: ...Hehe, there's no one else here, Pela — no need for such formality.

t_ch: …好的，布洛妮娅小姐。
t_en: ...Okay, Miss Bronya.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我听说你之前已经帮忙找回了不少贵重的展品，而且还作为兼职经理运营了博物馆一段时间——关于这起连环失窃案，你有什么见解吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, I heard that you helped recover a number of valuable items for the museum, and that you've been running the museum as a part-time manager — do you have any insights into this serial theft case?

t_ch: 走一步看一步，没什么头绪。
t_en: I'm just taking it one step at a time. There doesn't seem to be much headway.

t_ch: 是这样啊…我了解了。
t_en: Is that so? I see.

t_ch: 总觉得我们的运气出奇地好。
t_en: It feels like we've gotten very lucky.

t_ch: 可能这也是对你们付出努力的奖赏吧？
t_en: Or maybe it's just the fruit of your efforts?

t_ch: 背后的家伙肯定不简单。
t_en: Whoever's behind this is one Smooth Criminal for sure.

t_ch: 嗯，关于这点，我们可以达成共识。
t_en: Well, on that point, we can agree.

t_ch: 无论如何，这场闹剧已经在我们眼皮底下发生太久了。不管幕后的组织者有多神通广大，我们都必须把他给揪出来，向市民证明筑城者的决心。
t_en: In any case, this fiasco has been going on right under our noses for too long. Regardless of how cunning and talented the culprit may be, we must draw them out and demonstrate the resolve of the Architects.

t_ch: 佩拉，{NICKNAME}——我马上会发布指令，动员所有城区内执勤的银鬃铁卫展开上门调查。
t_en: Pela, {NICKNAME} — I will issue an immediate directive to mobilize all on-duty Silvermane Guards in the city to conduct a door-to-door investigation.

t_ch: 你们在行政区应该认识不少熟人，对吧？如果可以的话，我希望两位能在市中心广场附近查访一下行人，看看能不能采到一鳞半爪的线索。
t_en: You're familiar with many of the locals in the Administrative District, right? If possible, I'd like you to head to the city center and see if they have any clues to offer us.

t_ch: 明白了！我们这就出发，布洛妮娅小姐。
t_en: Understood! We'll head there at once, Miss Bronya.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，那就辛苦你继续和我一起搭档调查啦。我们到喷泉广场上碰头吧。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, guess you're stuck with me again for this assignment. Let's meet up at Fountain Plaza.

t_ch: 这里的人流不断…从哪里开始调查比较好呢？
t_en: This place is an endless stream of people... Where do we start?

t_ch: 先从熟人开始吧。
t_en: Let's start with people we already know.

t_ch: 对，我们看法一致，这样做的效率最高。
t_en: Agreed — that'll be more efficient.

t_ch: 我记得有几个人平时一直在广场附近放风…就从他们开始问起吧。
t_en: I remember lots of folks like to relax around the plaza. Let's head there first.

t_ch: 一个一个全问过去吧！
t_en: Let's interrogate them all one by one!

t_ch: …那怎么行，就算是搞普查也得讲究方法呀，否则效率就太低了。
t_en: ...That won't do. Even a survey requires rigorous methodology, let alone an investigation.

t_ch: 还是先从熟面孔开始吧，我记得有几个人平时一直在这附近放风…就从他们开始问起吧。
t_en: We can start with people we already know. I remember lots of folks like to relax around the plaza. Let's head there first.

t_ch: 冬城的孩子，「存护」骄傲的子民，抬起你们的头来！不要被忙碌且庸俗的日常磨平了棱角，时刻铭记筑城者和大守护者的教导！
t_en: Children of the Everwinter City, proud citizens of the Preservation: Hold your heads up high! Grind NOT against the mundane vulgarities of your everyday lives. ALWAYS remember, deep in your hearts, the teachings of the Architects and the Supreme Guardian!

t_ch: 眼前的男人醉心于他精心编织的演讲内容，似乎完全没有意识到你的到来。
t_en: The man before you seems so engrossed in the elaborate speech performance that he doesn't seem to have noticed your arrival.

t_ch: 你知道文物失窃的事吗？
t_en: Do you know anything about the stolen museum artifacts?

t_ch: 哦，这位听众莫非是…贝洛伯格的救星，现任守护者的贵宾，银河与星星之子！欢迎你回到这座纯白且美丽的冬城！
t_en: Oh, if it isn't... the savior of Belobog, the guardian's honored guest, child of the galaxy and stars! Welcome back to our eternally snow-covered yet forever beautiful winter metropolis!

t_ch: 那个…请您回答一下我们刚才的提问？
t_en: Um... Can you please answer the question we just asked?

t_ch: …不，我没听说过文物失窃的事。我每天都忙于在此地赞颂「存护」的恩典，并无闲暇旁骛世俗之事！
t_en: ...I haven't heard of any stolen artifacts. These days I'm too busy praising the grace of the Preservation to be distracted by worldly matters!

t_ch: 不过，若你们正被此事困扰得心神不宁，那我希望克里珀能将祂的赐福施于二位，愿你们早日找到犯下盗掠之罪的恶人！
t_en: However, if your hearts are troubled by this affair, then I hope that Qlipoth will bestow THEIR blessings upon you both, and that you may soon find the evildoer who committed such a crime!

t_ch: 呃…多谢您的祝福。
t_en: Uhh... Thanks for your well-wishes.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我们走吧。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, let's move on.

t_ch: 我好像记得你，年轻人。怎么，读过防骗教程后有问题想问了吗？
t_en: I remember you. Do you have any questions after reading the scam prevention guide?

t_ch: 还是说，你也想加入「深蓝骗局受害者互助会」？
t_en: ...Perhaps, you've decided to join the Dark-Blue Scam Victims Association?

t_ch: 你知道文物失窃的事吗？
t_en: Do you know anything about the stolen museum artifacts?

t_ch: 怎么会不知道？历史文化博物馆的珍贵展品逐一失窃，银鬃铁卫花费巨大精力搜查仍未能抓住幕后黑手……
t_en: How could I not? Precious items from the History and Culture Museum have been stolen one after another, and the culprit remains at large despite the Silvermane Guards' noblest efforts to track them down...

t_ch: 不瞒你们说，我这两天正打算去找人告发这次案件的始作俑者！
t_en: I'll tell you the truth — I've been preparing to publicly accuse the person responsible for this whole thing for two days now!

t_ch: …咦？这么说来，你手上掌握了确凿的犯罪证据？
t_en: Huh? Wait, so you have incriminating evidence against the culprit?

t_ch: 什么证不证据，这还需要证据吗？告诉你们吧，全贝洛伯格能干出这种事的只有那一个人——就是那个不要脸的蓝毛诈骗犯！
t_en: Evidence? Who needs evidence? Let me tell you, there is only one person in all of Belobog who can do such a thing — that shameless blue-haired fraudster!
Oh...

t_ch: 你们是来查案的？那正好，我这就给你们列举一下那个骗子的罪行——
t_en: You guys are here to investigate the case? Perfect, I'll give you a list of the culprit's scams, too—

t_ch: …这位先生，抱歉，我们今天只是来调查展品失窃案的…而且我们办案讲求证据，如果您有什么更确凿的线索，不妨联系执勤的铁卫……
t_en: Sorry sir, today we're only here to investigate the museum thefts... and we can't close the case without evidence. If you come across any reliable clues, please report them to an on-duty Silvermane Guard...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我们快走吧……
t_en: {NICKNAME}, let's get going.

t_ch: 您打算在请愿表上签字了吗？还是说，您有其它问题想问呢？
t_en: Ready to sign the petition? Have any other questions?

t_ch: 你知道文物失窃的事吗？
t_en: Do you know anything about the stolen museum artifacts?

t_ch: …啊，您是说历史文化博物馆的展品莫名连续失踪的那件事？之前略有耳闻。
t_en: ...Ah, you mean the items that have been disappearing mysteriously from the History and Culture Museum? I heard tell.

t_ch: 不知道《雪国冒险奇谭》的作者手稿有没有被盗走……
t_en: I wonder if they got their hands on the original manuscripts of Tale of the Winterlands...

t_ch: 绝对没有，请放心！我会拿生命保护那些手稿的！
t_en: Absolutely not! Don't worry, ma'am — I will protect those manuscripts with my life!

t_ch: …哇，好的，那我就放心了。您不用这么激动……
t_en: Oh, wow, that's a relief to hear. I didn't realize you were so passionate...

t_ch: …咳，不好意思。还是回归正题吧，您有任何能与文物失窃案联系上的线索吗？或者，您最近有没有察觉到身边有什么异样？
t_en: ...Ahem, sorry about that. Back to the topic at hand — do you have any clues that could be linked to the theft of the museum's artifacts? Have you noticed anything unusual lately?

t_ch: 嗯，让我回忆一下…编辑部最近在招人，新来的实习责编被主编骂了几次，其它还有……
t_en: Well, let me think... the editorial department has been recruiting recently — the new editor intern was scolded by the editor-in-chief a few times, and then...

t_ch: 唔，抱歉，尽是些不足挂齿的小事…我这里好像真的没有什么值得注意的线索，要不两位还是去问问广场上的其他人吧？
t_en: Sorry, these are all everyday occurrences... I really don't know of any noteworthy clues. Maybe you could try asking the other people around here?

t_ch: 这样啊…我明白了，不好意思占用了你的时间。
t_en: I see, sorry for taking up your time.

t_ch: 我们走吧，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: Let's move along then, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 唔…又是半天过去了，结果还是一无所获……
t_en: *sigh* Another half day gone, and nothing to show for it...

t_ch: 可恶，躲在影子里的家伙…难道我们真的连一点把柄都抓不着吗？
t_en: Ugh, the culprit is still hiding in the shadows. Can't we find even one lead?

t_ch: 唷！美丽的贝洛伯格人民公仆，还有神通广大的外来者，你们好啊！
t_en: Ah! If it isn't Belobog's most adorable public servant — and the magnificent outsider! Good day to you!

t_ch: …是谁？
t_en: ...You know us?

t_ch: 这位优雅的小女士，我经常见你在永冬铭碑附近向孩子们科普贝洛伯格的历史知识，你的博识和耐心令我真心感到敬佩！
t_en: Young lady, I have seen you on many occasions near the Everwinter Monument, teaching children the history of Belobog. I am truly in awe of your knowledge and patience!

t_ch: 唔，女士就女士，为什么前面还要加个「小」…不过还是承蒙夸奖啦。请问您是？
t_en: Hehe, okay... thanks for the compliment, I guess. May I ask, with whom do we have the pleasure of speaking?

t_ch: 我是华劳斯•凯特博瑞屈•莱姆士汀，莱姆士汀家族第二十五任家主。很荣幸有机会与你交谈！
t_en: I am Wallace Cambridge Limestein, the <unbreak>25</unbreak>th head of the Limestein family. I am honored to make your acquaintance!

t_ch: 你旁边的这位外来者我也很熟悉了，毕竟{F#她}{M#他}经常从我面前穿过，还很少跟我打招呼。
t_en: And yes, I am familiar with this outsider next to you — {F#she}{M#he} often walks right past me without so much as a greeting.

t_ch: 你这么记仇？
t_en: Are you holding a grudge!?

t_ch: 怎么能说是记仇呢！我只是希望身边的每一个人都能掌握基本的礼数罢了。
t_en: How could you say such a thing!? I just want everyone to have a proper understanding of basic manners.

t_ch: 我不记得你…
t_en: I Don't Even Know Who You Are!

t_ch: 我是华劳斯•凯特博瑞屈•莱姆士汀，莱姆士汀家族第二十五任……
t_en: I am Wallace Cambridge Limestein, the <unbreak>25</unbreak>th head of the Lime—

t_ch: …好了好了，就此打住。莱姆士汀先生，您是有什么话想跟我们说吗？
t_en: Alright, alright, we got it. Mr. Limestein, is there something you want to discuss with us?

t_ch: 哦，正是——我看二位已经在这行政广场附近徘徊了许久，逢人便要停下来询问，想必是在调查什么要紧之事。
t_en: Oh, as a matter of fact, I do. I noticed you two had been wandering around Administrative District Plaza for some time now, stopping to ask questions at every turn, so I surmised you were investigating something important.

t_ch: 恰巧，最近本人身边发生了一件怪事，令我颇为疑惑…思考良久，我觉得还是该把这件事告知公职人员最为妥当。
t_en: As it so happens, I witnessed a strange incident recently that left me quite puzzled... After reflecting on it for a time, I decided it would be most appropriate to inform public officials.

t_ch: 您说的这件事…和博物馆的展品失窃案有关系吗？
t_en: This incident... is it related to the museum thefts?

t_ch: 谈不上有直接联系，但我那位失联的朋友以前确实喜好逛展，也经常和我们一众茶友聊起相关的话题。
t_en: I can't say for sure that it's directly related, but a friend of mine I no longer hear from used to frequent the museum, and often talked about its exhibits with a group of us tea lovers.

t_ch: 唔，您说「失联」，意思是您现在已经联系不上这位朋友了？
t_en: So by "no longer hear from," you mean you've been unable to get in touch with this friend of yours?

t_ch: 没错，正是！我那位朋友名叫诺尔伯特，我们以前经常在歌德宾馆的大堂共享午歇时光——当然了，还有其他几名上流人士结伴。
t_en: Precisely! His name is Norbert — we used to chat during lunch breaks in the lobby of the Goethe Hotel — along with a few other high-class individuals, of course.

t_ch: 但不知从哪天起，诺尔伯特突然不来参加茶会了，整个人仿佛人间蒸发了一般。我还记得他最后跟我说过的话，大概意思是……
t_en: And one day, Norbert suddenly stopped showing up to our tea parties. It was as though he disappeared into thin air. I still remember the last thing he said to me...

t_ch: 「莱姆士汀，听我一句——人一辈子总得有点野心，干一次大事！」
t_en: "Limestein, listen to me — one must be ambitious in life. I'm gonna do something big for once!"

t_ch: 「有点野心，干一次大事」…从有钱的公子哥嘴里说出来，感觉不太对劲啊。
t_en: "Be ambitious in life, do something big for once..." Coming from the mouth of a wealthy gentleman, that sounded rather curious to me.

t_ch: 这位诺尔伯特先生，您知道他是从事什么职业的吗？
t_en: This Norbert guy, do you know what his line of work was?

t_ch: 不，我不清楚，也从来没细问过。
t_en: I'm not entirely sure — I never asked.

t_ch: 你们不是朋友吗？
t_en: Weren't the two of you friends?

t_ch: 这你就不懂了，君子之交淡如水，大家都是有头有脸的人物，何必纠结这些琐碎杂事？
t_en: You wouldn't understand — friendship between gentlemen is as pure as water. We are all dignified people, why bother with such trivial matters?

t_ch: 原来只是酒肉朋友…
t_en: So more like a drinking buddy than a friend...

t_ch: 这叫什么话！所谓君子之交淡如水，大家都是有头有脸的人物，何必纠结这些琐碎杂事？
t_en: What a preposterous statement! You wouldn't understand — friendship between gentlemen is as pure as water. We are all dignified people, why bother with such trivial matters?

t_ch: 呃…在大多数人眼里，「职业」和「琐碎杂事」还是有很大区别的……
t_en: Ugh... In the eyes of most people, there's a world of difference between "trivial matters" and a career.

t_ch: …你提供的线索我都记录下来了，莱姆士汀先生。感谢您对银鬃铁卫的信任，后续调查如果有任何进展，我们会及时通知您的。
t_en: ...I have recorded all the clues you provided, Mr. Limestein. Thank you for putting your trust in the Silvermane Guards. We will keep you informed if there are any developments in the investigation.

t_ch: 哪里哪里，我才应该感谢你呢，铁卫小女士。那么，我就先行告辞了。
t_en: No, no, I should be thanking you, young lady of the Guards. Well then, if that's all, I shall take my leave.

t_ch: 不知道这位华劳斯先生的「朋友」跟展品失窃案有没有瓜葛……
t_en: I wonder if this "friend" of Mr. Wallace has anything to do with the museum thefts...

t_ch: 我们今天已经调查得够久了，再问下去恐怕也只是白白耗费精力。{NICKNAME}，我们回去和布洛妮娅大人汇报一下吧。
t_en: That's enough detective work for today — questioning more people will just be a waste of energy. {NICKNAME}, let's go report back to Lady Bronya.

t_ch: 布洛妮娅小姐，我们回来了。
t_en: Miss Bronya, we're back.

t_ch: 辛苦二位了。如何，你们有从行政区的市民那里打听出什么吗？
t_en: Thank you both for your hard work. Did you find out anything in the Administrative District?

t_ch: 没有获取到跟文物盗窃案直接相关的线索，但有一件事值得留意……
t_en: We didn't find anything directly related to the museum thefts, but there was one thing worth noting...

t_ch: 将华劳斯的「朋友」失联一事转告给了布洛妮娅……
t_en: Tell Bronya about Wallace's disappearing "friend."

t_ch: 原来如此，是莱姆士汀家的少爷提供的消息啊。
t_en: I see. It was the man from the Limestein family who provided the information.

t_ch: 诺尔伯特…诺尔伯特…总觉得在哪里听过这个名字。不好意思，请给我点时间回想一下……
t_en: Norbert... Norbert... I feel like I've heard that name somewhere before. Please, give me a moment to see if I can recall...

t_ch: …啊，我想起来了。交通部大臣诺伊尔有一个侄子就叫诺尔伯特，现在应该是在市政部门做运输协调的工作。
t_en: ...Ah, I remember now. The Minister of Transportation, Noel, has a nephew named Norbert — I believe he's currently working in the Municipal Department as a transportation coordinator.

t_ch: 这你都能记住？！
t_en: You can remember all of that!?

t_ch: 一城之主理应熟悉下属的身份和分工，不必惊讶。诺尔伯特的工作的确算是份闲职，难怪他经常和莱姆士汀家的公子来往。
t_en: The leader of a city should be familiar with the status and division of labor of her subordinates — that's just part of the job. Norbert's position is indeed an idle one, so it's no wonder he has time to socialize with members of the Limestein family.

t_ch: 果然也是个公子哥。
t_en: Guess he comes from old money too?

t_ch: 他那份工作的确称得上闲职，也难怪经常会和莱姆士汀家的公子来往。
t_en: Norbert's position is indeed an idle one — it's no wonder he has time to socialize with members of the Limestein family.

t_ch: 如果诺尔伯特真的如华劳斯所说，已经连续几天不见踪影了，他身边一定有侍者知道那位公子的去向。
t_en: If what Wallace said is right, then Norbert must have been missing for several days already. There must be someone who knows where he is.

t_ch: 就算他们想为自家少爷保密…只要稍加敲打就一定会招供。
t_en: Even if they want to keep the young man's secret... they'll cough it up with the right kind of encouragement.

t_ch: 凯尔，这件事能拜托你吗？我想麻烦你去造访一下诺伊尔大臣的府邸，四处询问一下这位诺尔伯特公子的下落。
t_en: Kyle, can I ask you a favor? I would like you to visit the residence of Minister Noel and ask around regarding the whereabouts of Mr. Norbert.

t_ch: 遵命，守护者大人！
t_en: Understood, Madam Guardian!

t_ch: 一段时间过后，卫兵凯尔回到了克里珀堡……
t_en: After some time, Kyle heads back to Qlipoth Fort...

t_ch: 卫兵凯尔向布洛妮娅、主角和佩拉汇报他得到的消息。
t_en: Kyle reports back to Bronya, Pela, and the Trailblazer.

t_ch: 布洛妮娅大人，佩拉副官，我回来了。
t_en: Lady Bronya, Miss Pela, I have returned.

t_ch: 动作很快啊，凯尔。从交通部大臣那里打听到什么了吗？
t_en: You were quite expedient, Kyle. Did you find anything out from the Department of Transportation?

t_ch: 稍微费了点功夫，但有几个胆子小的侍从很快就开口了——
t_en: It took a little prodding, but a few of the more timid attendants started speaking up...

t_ch: 他们说诺尔伯特公子最近的确经常在奇怪的时间点出门，每次被问到的时候总是支支吾吾地说自己要去商店街「踩点」。
t_en: They say that Mr. Norbert was indeed leaving at odd hours recently. Whenever someone would ask where he was headed, he'd mumble something about going to the shopping street to "inspect the site."

t_ch: 侍从都以为他是背着长辈在和谁偷偷约会，所以也没有把这件事通报给交通部大臣。
t_en: The attendants think he might have a love interest — someone he doesn't want his family to know about — they haven't mentioned anything to the Minister of Transportation.

t_ch: 奇怪的时间点，「商店街」…难道……
t_en: Going out at unusual hours, the shopping street, could it be...

t_ch: …佩拉，{NICKNAME}——我有一个请求，能麻烦两位前去<color=#dbc291ff>边缘通路</color>探查一下那里的情况吗？
t_en: ...Pela, {NICKNAME} — I have a request. I'd like the two of you to scout out <color=#dbc291ff>Backwater Pass</color> and see whether anything's happening there.

t_ch: 为什么去那里？
t_en: Why there?

t_ch: 诺尔伯特和案件有关系吗？
t_en: Does Norbert have anything to do with our investigation?

t_ch: 两位忙着调查流动人群的时候，我也收到了几条线报。有消息源说，最近目击到可疑人员偷偷出入裂界侵蚀的街区，而且居然没有被铁卫拦下。
t_en: While the two of you were busy questioning people in the city, I received some tip-offs. One source said that suspicious people were recently seen sneaking around the Fragmentum corrosion zone, and that the Silvermane Guards didn't prevent them from entering.

t_ch: 结合诺尔伯特慌张之中脱口而出的「商店街」…我觉得，所有这些线索大概可以合为一束了。
t_en: Together with Norbert's mumblings about a "shopping street..." I think all these clues are pointing in one direction.

t_ch: 这么说来，这位诺尔伯特公子真的很有可能是我们要找的文物大盗咯？
t_en: ...So this Mr. Norbert could well be the museum thief we're looking for?

t_ch: …那就一刻都不能再等了！{NICKNAME}，我们赶紧出发去逮捕那个家伙吧。
t_en: ...We can't hold off a moment longer! Hurry, {NICKNAME}, let's go arrest that scoundrel.

t_ch: 抱歉，我现在还有诸多事务缠身，不便与你们同去。祝你们一切顺利，找到失窃的贵重展品。
t_en: My apologies, I have a lot of business to attend to at present — I won't be joining you. Good luck recovering the stolen items.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，事不宜迟，我们这就出发吧。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, we can't delay a moment longer. We need to move now.

t_ch: 出发吧。
t_en: Let's head out.

t_ch: 稍等一下。
t_en: I need a little while.

t_ch: 好，你再多准备准备，不过别花太久了。
t_en: ...Okay, whenever you're ready, but don't take too long!

t_ch: 归还胶片
t_en: Return the microfilm.

t_ch: …成功了！幻灯片播放一切正常，看来微缩胶片没有被损坏。
t_en: ...It works! The slides play without a hitch — the microfilm is undamaged.

t_ch: 投影仪复位以后，<color=#dbc291ff>历史区也可以对市民开放了</color>。这样一来，博物馆最主要的三个展区都可以正常接待游客了！
t_en: Once the projector is restored, <color=#dbc291ff>the History-Culture Hall can reopen to the public</color>. Then visitors can enjoy all three of the museum's most important exhibits!

t_ch: 我要再次郑重替所有博物馆的工作人员、还有贝洛伯格的市民感谢你，{NICKNAME}！没有你的帮助，恐怕这座馆里的很多展品都要销声匿迹了。
t_en: I want to thank you again, {NICKNAME}, on behalf of all the museum staff, and all the citizens of Belobog! If it weren't for your help, I'm afraid many of the exhibits in this museum would have been lost.

t_ch: 别太客气了。
t_en: Don't mention it.

t_ch: 才不是客气！在我看来，现在开放这座博物馆的意义十分重大…比过去任何一次都更值得铭记。
t_en: I'm just stating the obvious! The way I see it, the museum's reopening is a very significant event... and worth remembering more than any other time in the past.

t_ch: 再给我头上记一笔大功！
t_en: Another great accomplishment to my name!

t_ch: 嘿嘿，那是当然。你知道吗？在我看来，现在开放这座博物馆的意义十分重大…比过去任何一次都更值得铭记。
t_en: Hehe, that's for sure. And you know what? The way I see it, the museum's reopening is a very significant event... and worth remembering more than any other time in the past.

t_ch: 过去的十几年里，下层区有整整一代人成长了起来，但他们却从来没有机会接触到这些珍贵的艺术品和历史真迹…你的所作所为帮他们填补上了巨大的空洞。
t_en: A whole generation has grown up in the Underworld in the last decade or so, and they never had the privilege of seeing these precious works of art and historical treasures... what you've done has helped bridge a huge gap for them.

t_ch: 所以，哪怕你自己没什么感觉——但你真的做了一件非常伟大的事哦。
t_en: So, even if it seems small to you — you did a very great thing here.

t_ch: 哎呀，我说这些干嘛，真是…那个，如果你想继续担任博物馆的兼职经理、负责历史区的日常经营，就去和艾丽斯小姐说一声吧？
t_en: Ahem, that's enough praise for now... Anyway, if you did want to continue working as a part-time manager and handle the daily operations of the History-Culture Hall, I recommend talking to Miss Eris.

t_ch: 反正现在也没有别的合适人选。你能帮我们这个忙，顺便自己也乐在其中，不是挺好的嘛？
t_en: It's not like we don't have any other suitable candidates for the position... Isn't it great you can help us with such an enjoyable job?

t_ch: …啊，对了。诺尔伯特少爷那边，如果我们从他嘴里撬出什么值得留意的信息，我也会及时通知你的。回见啦，{NICKNAME}！
t_en: ...Ah, by the way, I'll keep you informed if we pry anything noteworthy out of that Mr. Norbert guy. See you later, {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 唔…感觉不太对劲。明明是已经废弃的商业街，但总感觉这里刚刚有很多人来过……
t_en: Uh... something doesn't feel right. It's obviously an abandoned shopping street, but it also feels like people were here not too long ago...

t_ch: 小心点，{NICKNAME}。不管犯人是不是那位诺尔伯特少爷…他肯定都不缺帮手。
t_en: We must be cautious, {NICKNAME}. Whether the culprit is Norbert or someone else... he certainly has no shortage of helpers.

t_ch: 嘘…！{NICKNAME}，里面有人在说话，我们听听看！
t_en: Shh...! {NICKNAME}, there are people talking inside. Let's see if we can hear them!

t_ch: …可是、可是少爷，假扮银鬃铁卫可是大罪一件啊……
t_en: ...B—But sir, pretending to be a Silvermane Guard is a serious crime...

t_ch: 你怎么怂得跟个新手一样，就这点出息还想挣大钱？
t_en: Stop acting like a wimp! You think you can make a fortune with that kind of attitude?

t_ch: 别想那么多，放好你的哨！今天的口令是<color=#dbc291ff>不烤面包烤红肠</color>，记住没？
t_en: Don't overthink things — just play your part. Today's password is "<color=#dbc291ff>roast the sausage, not the bread</color>." You got that?

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>不烤面包烤红肠</color>…好，记住了，记住了。
t_en: Got it, sir. <color=#dbc291ff>Roast the sausage, not the bread</color>.

t_ch: 对不上口令的都不许放进来，哪怕是只虫子也不行！听见没有？
t_en: Don't let anything in that doesn't know the password — not even a fly! You got that?

t_ch: 听、听见了，您放心吧，诺尔伯特少爷！
t_en: Don't worry Mr. Norbert, I understand.

t_ch: 果然是那个诺尔伯特！他居然还让同伙假扮成铁卫，真是胆大包天！
t_en: So it is Norbert after all! And he's making his accomplice impersonate a Silvermane Guard! The audacity!

t_ch: …这下可算被我们找到了，{NICKNAME}！今天我们一定要抓他个现行！
t_en: We've found the culprit, {NICKNAME}! Now we just have to catch him red-handed!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，等等，先别走！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, wait, stay still!

t_ch: 我好像听到有人在铁门后面说话，我们先去听听他们在盘算什么吧。
t_en: I think I heard someone talking behind the metal gate. Let's see if we can hear what they're saying.

s_en: Criminal Accomplice Posing as Silvermane Guard
t_ch: 喂，你们！站住！
t_en: Hey, you two! Don't move!

t_ch: 肯定是那个家伙的从犯…{NICKNAME}，我们机灵点！
t_en: Must be the accomplice... {NICKNAME}, let's think fast!

s_en: Criminal Accomplice Posing as Silvermane Guard
t_ch: 报出通行口令，否则就滚远点！这里不是小市民逛街的地方！
t_en: Say the password or get out of here! This isn't any old shopping street, y'know!

t_ch: 不煮皮蛋煮鱼汤。
t_en: Boil the fish, not the egg.

s_en: Criminal Accomplice Posing as Silvermane Guard
t_ch: …什么乱七八糟的？赶紧回家吧，我们在忙着交…咳，在忙着执行公务呢，闲杂人等别来捣乱！
t_en: ...Wh—What's that supposed to mean? You'd best turn around and go home — we're busy handling the delivery of— Ahem! B—Busy handlin— um... executing official business. No loitering!

t_ch: 等等，我记得口令！「不烤面包烤红肠」，是这个吧？
t_en: Wait, I remember! Roast the sausage, not the bread. That's the password, right?

t_ch: 不烤面包烤红肠。
t_en: Roast the sausage, not the bread.

t_ch: 佩拉，救我…
t_en: Help me, Pela...

t_ch: 咦？哦对，口令，口令…「不烤面包烤红肠」，是这个吧？
t_en: What? Oh right, the password... Roast the sausage, not the bread. That's the password, right?

s_en: Criminal Accomplice Posing as Silvermane Guard
t_ch: 啊？居然答对了……
t_en: Huh? I... Y—Yes, that's correct...

t_ch: 嗯哼，这下可以放我们进去了吧？我们要和诺尔伯特少爷聊要紧事，和生意有关。
t_en: Mm-hmm! So, you are gonna let us in now? We need to talk to Mr. Norbert about some important business-related matters.

s_en: Criminal Accomplice Posing as Silvermane Guard
t_ch: 等等，你刚才说…诺尔伯特少爷？
t_en: Wait, did you just say... Mr. Norbert?

s_en: Criminal Accomplice Posing as Silvermane Guard
t_ch: 你们两个，到底是谁？！这次行动从头到尾老大都没露过面，凭几个外人是怎么知道他的名字的？
t_en: Who are you guys!? This whole operation the boss hasn't shown his face once. How could a couple of outsiders just walk in knowing his name?

t_ch: 百密一疏…这下糟了，{NICKNAME}……
t_en: Looks like our cover's blown, {NICKNAME}...

t_ch: 没办法，看来想通过这里就只能来硬的了！
t_en: Guess we'll have to do this the hard way!

t_ch: 继续往前走的话，估计还会遇到更多给诺尔伯特打工的家伙。
t_en: We'll probably end up running into more of Norbert's goons up ahead.

t_ch: 一定要多加小心，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: We should proceed with caution, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 快看，又是诺尔伯特！
t_en: Look, it's Norbert again!

t_ch: 听听他这回又有什么要说的吧。
t_en: Let's see what he's talking about this time.

t_ch: …胶片呢？货都准备好了吗？
t_en: ...And the film? Is it packed and ready to go?

t_ch: 都在这儿呢，少爷！买家就快到了，您往这边走！
t_en: Right here, boss! The buyer is arriving soon. Here, this way!

t_ch: 很好，很好，干得不错。耐心点，马上咱们都要发大财咯。
t_en: Very good, very good. Well done. Patience, soon we will all be rich.

t_ch: 胶片！他刚才提到了胶片，你也听到了吧？肯定就是咱们要找的那一卷。
t_en: Film! Did you hear that? That film he mentioned must be the same one that was stolen from the museum.

t_ch: 这家伙，明明家里那么有钱，居然还会起这种歪心思——简直不可理喻！我们得想办法阻止这场交易！
t_en: Why would someone from such a wealthy family do something like this for money... It's unbelievable! We have to find a way to stop this before they sell the film!

t_ch: 等等，{NICKNAME}！先别急着上去。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, wait, don't make a move yet!

t_ch: 为了防止那家伙抵赖，最好还是先拍张照片作为证据。
t_en: We should take some pictures as evidence in case the culprit denies the charges.

t_ch: 用你的设备拍吧？清晰度应该更好些。
t_en: Your equipment probably has better specs than mine — I'll leave the picture-taking to you.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，回来！别这么着急！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, come back! We're not done!

t_ch: 先拍张照片，留下证据再上去吧。
t_en: Take a picture first for evidence. Then we can make a move.

t_ch: 克鲁斯帕家的诺尔伯特，还有在他旁边的家伙——转过来，面对我！把手放在我能看得见的地方！
t_en: Norbert of the Cusper family, and everyone else here — turn around and face me! Put your hands where I can see them!

t_ch: …怎、怎么回事？！你们两个是谁？
t_en: Wh—What's going on? Who are you two?

t_ch: 我们是来把你绳之以法的！
t_en: We're here to bring you to justice!

t_ch: 华劳斯托我向你问好！
t_en: Wallace says hi!

t_ch: 这是什么情况，诺尔伯特？！你不是说过，安保工作万无一失吗？
t_en: What's going on here, Norbert!? Didn't you say your security was impenetrable?

t_ch: 我的手下…不是，铁卫！赶紧过来把这两个闯入者赶走！
t_en: Minions— I—I mean, Silvermane Guards! Get rid of these intruders immediately!

t_ch: 敢在银鬃铁卫的情报官面前耀武扬威，我看你们真是活腻了！
t_en: You've got a lot of nerve drawing your weapons in front of an Intelligence Officer of the Silvermane Guards!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我们上！让这些冒牌货吃点苦头！
t_en: Come on, {NICKNAME}! Let's give these imposters a taste of justice!

t_ch: 别挣扎了，诺尔伯特少爷——你的从犯已经被我们制服，现在没地方跑了！
t_en: There's no point in resisting, Mr. Norbert. Your accomplices have been subdued. There's nowhere to run.

t_ch: 你好大的胆子，居然还敢教唆手下乔装成银鬃铁卫…我倒要看看裁判团会怎么给你量罪。
t_en: You had the audacity to tell your men to disguise themselves as Silvermane Guards... I'll be interested to see what kind of sentence the adjudication panel gives you.

t_ch: 唔…你们…这…不对，你们没有证据！我们只是在这里……
t_en: I... You... Th—This isn't right — you have no evidence of wrongdoing! We're just here to—

t_ch: 还想抵赖？你们秘密交易的过程已经被我们拍下来了，证据都在我们手上。还有什么想说的？
t_en: Still in denial? Your secret dealings have been photographed. We have all the evidence we need. Is there anything else you want to say?

t_ch: 我…呜哇……
t_en: I... *whimper*

t_ch: 我、我就知道不该信你！你们这帮纨绔子弟，各个成事不足败事有余！
t_en: I—I knew I couldn't trust you hooligans! Even the simplest of sales was too much for you to handle!

t_ch: …好铁卫妹妹，我投降，我跟你走！这卷「微缩胶带」也还给你，把它还给博物馆吧，对不起！
t_en: Silvermane Guard lady, I surrender! Take the microfilm back to the museum! I'm sorry!

t_ch: 我只求你一件事，别把我交到我叔叔手上！克里珀在上，他肯定会气得把我的皮扒下来的！
t_en: I only ask one thing — please don't hand me over to my uncle! With Qlipoth as my witness, uncle would be so angry he'd turn me into a leather jacket!

t_ch: 哼哼，这可就不由我说了算了……
t_en: Hmph, that's not up to me...

t_ch: 这一路下来可太不容易了，{NICKNAME}！结果真是皆大欢喜，不仅找回了「微缩胶片」，还抓住了文物失窃案的始作俑者…这可是大功一件呀。
t_en: It was a difficult journey, {NICKNAME}, but it had a happy ending! Not only did we manage to recover the microfilm, but we also caught the culprit behind the museum thefts... That's something worth celebrating.

t_ch: 不过，我还是稍微有些迷惑…这位诺尔伯特少爷不过是个鬼迷心窍的公子哥，他哪来的那么大本事，召集了这么多人手，还组织了这么大规模的盗宝行动……
t_en: However, I'm still a little confused... Mr. Norbert is wealthy and opportunistic, but how did he manage to find so many accomplices and organize such a large-scale smuggling operation...

t_ch: …不过这些问题还是留到审讯的时候再问吧，你我的工作到这一步就算告一段落啦。这一路以来也辛苦你帮忙了，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: ...These questions can wait until the interrogation. As for you and me, our work ends here. Thanks for your efforts, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 走吧，我们把「微缩胶片」带回博物馆去。
t_en: Come on, let's take the microfilm back to the museum.

t_ch: 可恶，可恶啊！老早就有人提醒我别亲自掺和这摊浑水，割点肉找个代理……
t_en: Curse it, curse it all! He warned me not to carry out the transaction in person, but I didn't want to spend money on hiring a bagman...

t_ch: 我不想蹲大牢啊，一天都不想！
t_en: I don't want to go to prison! Not even for a day!

t_ch: 哼，干了坏事还想不受惩罚，做梦！
t_en: Hmph, if you think you can commit crimes and go unpunished, dream on!

t_ch: 呜…「寒腿叔叔」，这种时候该怎么办…救救我啊！
t_en: *whimper* ...Mr. Cold Feet, what should I do in a situation like this? Save me, save me!

t_ch: …你嘀咕什么呢，什么「叔叔」？
t_en: What are you talking about? Who's got cold feet?

t_ch: 呜…没、没什么，我自言自语罢了……
t_en: Ah... N—Nothing, I was just talking to myself....

t_ch: 我的人生…不会就这么毁了吧？呜……
t_en: I had my whole life ahead of me... is it all over? *whimper*

t_ch: 别哭了，早知如此，何必当初？每个人都得为自己做出的人生决定负责，没有例外。
t_en: Enough blubbing. If you didn't want to face the time, why do the crime? Everyone is responsible for the decisions they make in life — no exceptions.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，又见面啦，气色不错嘛。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, nice to see you. You're looking in good spirits.

t_ch: 博物馆现在真是人头攒动！我从来没见过游客如此爆满的情况，你可真是经营有方。
t_en: The museum is packed these days — I've never seen it so full of visitors. You run a tight ship here.

t_ch: 要是那些小朋友没有四处乱扔零食包装就更好了…这才多一会儿啊，我已经捡到三包吃到一半的雪地跳跳糖了。
t_en: Still, it'd be nice if those kids would stop littering... It's barely lunchtime and I've already picked up three half-eaten packs of snow-white popping candy.

t_ch: 又有什么事吗，佩拉？
t_en: Is there something you wanted to talk about, Pela?

t_ch: 啊，放心吧，这次没有东西被偷了。
t_en: Ah, don't worry, nothing was stolen this time.

t_ch: 还记得我们上次抓到的那位诺尔伯特公子吗？铁卫从他嘴里打听出了新情报，我这次是特地来通知你的。
t_en: You remember that Norbert guy we captured? The Silvermane Guards have acquired new intel from him — I thought you might find it interesting.

t_ch: 素质教育这一块还有待提升。
t_en: Kids these days...

t_ch: 我看也是，被我抓到的话一定得好好教训一顿。
t_en: I know, right? I'll teach them a lesson myself if I catch them in the act, that's for sure.

t_ch: 说回正事，你还记得上次抓到的那位诺尔伯特公子吧？铁卫从他嘴里打听出了新情报，我这次是特地来通知你的。
t_en: By the way, you remember that Norbert guy we captured? The Silvermane Guards have acquired new intel from him — I thought you might find it interesting.

t_ch: 我之前就一直在怀疑：他一个没什么社会经历的富家公子哥，是怎么突然想到要当文物大盗的？
t_en: I always wondered — how did he, a rich kid with virtually no real life experience, suddenly think of becoming a smuggler?

t_ch: 而且，只凭那家伙的知识面和阅历，根本不可能想出把「齿轮与智慧」拆成零碎的部件、再藏在机器人身体里这种点子。
t_en: There's no way someone like him could've come up with the idea of disassembling a sculpture and hiding the pieces inside robots.

t_ch: 结果也正如我所料，我们才审讯了不到半天的时间，那家伙就全招了。
t_en: The result is just as I expected... After less than half a day of interrogation, he confessed everything.

t_ch: 根据诺尔伯特的供词，他之所以会开始打博物馆展品的主意，完全是因为受了一个名叫<color=#dbc291ff>「寒腿叔叔」</color>的神秘人物煽动。
t_en: He admitted that a mysterious figure named <color=#dbc291ff>Mr. Cold Feet</color> persuaded him to target the museum exhibits.

t_ch: 「寒腿叔叔」忽悠他说，博物馆的展品有很多都是铁卫通过强征的手段从私人收藏家手里抢夺过去的，真正弘扬正义的行为是让文物回归民间……
t_en: Mr. Cold Feet fooled Norbert into believing that many of the museum's artifacts were forcibly seized from private collectors by the Silvermane Guards. He told him that true justice would be returning the artifacts to the people...

t_ch: …这种鬼话，诺尔伯特自己估计都不信。他只是想靠自己的「能力」赚一笔横财罢了。
t_en: Norbert probably didn't believe any of that nonsense — I think he just wanted to prove himself and add more to his fortune.

t_ch: 那位「寒腿叔叔」除了给他介绍买家之外，还会给他出谋划策，而且最后只收他三成的顾问费用…在咱们的大少爷看来，这笔生意可太划算了。
t_en: Mr. Cold Feet not only introduced him to buyers, but also gave him advice, and only charged him a <unbreak>30%</unbreak> consultant's fee... In the opinion of our young Mr. Norbert, the deal was too good to refuse.

t_ch: 找到这个「寒腿叔叔」了吗？
t_en: Did you find this Mr. Cold Feet?

t_ch: …咳，这就是我来找你的原因，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: ...Ahem. That's what I wanted to discuss with you, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 这么好的生意，怎么不找我…
t_en: Sounds lucrative. Why didn't he recruit me...

t_ch: …咳，我可以当作没听见喔。
t_en: ...Ahem. I'll pretend I didn't hear that.

t_ch: 话说回来，{NICKNAME}，我来找你就是为了这个「寒腿叔叔」。
t_en: So, {NICKNAME}, I came to discuss with you about this Mr. Cold Feet.

t_ch: 听诺尔伯特说，「寒腿叔叔」行事特别谨慎，而且对铁卫的一举一动了如指掌。只要城里稍有风声，他就会消失得无影无踪，根本找不到影子……
t_en: Norbert said Mr. Cold Feet operates with extreme discretion and knows the Silvermane Guards' every move like the back of his hand. If he catches wind about the slightest rumor in the city, he'll disappear without a trace.

t_ch: 如果那位少爷说的属实，保险起见，我们想派一位经验丰富的「民间人士」去尝试接触「寒腿叔叔」，看看能不能把他从巢穴里引出来。
t_en: If what Mr. Norbert said is true, then our safest option is to send a civilian to try to reach out to Mr. Cold Feet and lure him out of his lair.

t_ch: 你们想让我去会会他？
t_en: You want me to go and meet him?

t_ch: 对！大家都一致觉得，没有比你更合适的人选了。
t_en: You guessed it! Everyone agrees that there is no better candidate than you.

t_ch: 诺尔伯特说，他一般会<color=#dbc291ff>隔着歌德宾馆的房门</color>跟「寒腿叔叔」沟通。平常约定的会面时间…就是现在。
t_en: Norbert said he usually communicates with Mr. Cold Feet <color=#dbc291ff>through the door of a room in the Goethe Hotel</color>. The usual agreed meeting time is... right now.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，就把这当做来自铁卫的最后请求吧——能请你去歌德宾馆会会这位「寒腿叔叔」吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, take this as a final request from the Silvermane Guards — can you go to the Goethe Hotel to meet with this Mr. Cold Feet?

t_ch: 客房的房门纹丝不动，你感觉有一丝凉意略过自己的膝盖——要是在这冰冷的走廊里再多站一会儿，你自己恐怕就要变成{F#寒腿阿姨}{M#寒腿叔叔}了。
t_en: The door remains unperturbed, and you feel a chill breeze brush past your knees... If you stayed any longer in this freezing hallway, you would soon turn into a {F#Miss Cold Feet}{M#Mr. Cold Feet} yourself.

t_ch: 轻敲四次，重敲一次。
t_en: Tap four times and knock once.

t_ch: 房门内没有任何动静，也许你应该再试一次。
t_en: (There is no reaction from behind the door, perhaps you should try again.)

t_ch: 轻敲两次，重敲三次。
t_en: Tap twice and knock three times.

t_ch: 房门内没有任何动静，也许你应该再试一次。
t_en: (There is no reaction from behind the door, perhaps you should try again.)

t_ch: 轻敲三次，重敲两次。
t_en: Tap three times and knock twice.

t_ch: （什么动静都没有…是我记错暗号了吗？）
t_en: (There's no response... did I remember the wrong password?)

t_ch: 嗯？诺尔伯特，是你吗？
t_en: Hm? Norbert, is that you?

t_ch: （压低声音）对，是我。
t_en: (In a low voice) Yes, it's me.

t_ch: 倒霉催的，你怎么现在才来？！
t_en: *sigh* What took you so long?

t_ch: 不是，我是银鬃铁卫。
t_en: No, it's a Silvermane Guard.

t_ch: 哈、哈、哈。真好笑……
t_en: Haha... Someone write that for you?

t_ch: …就怪了！你个倒霉催的，怎么这个点才来？！
t_en: *sigh* Anyway, what took you so long?

t_ch: 我还以为你已经被铁卫给逮住了——刚才我还在寻思，以你小子的性格，落网以后岂不第一时间就会把我给供出来？
t_en: I was worried the Silvermane Guards might've got to you. But you're not the type to turn someone in at first capture, right?

t_ch: 那我可得赶紧跑路，反正这趟已经赚得盆满钵满了……
t_en: Well, it's time for me to get on out of here. After all, we already made a nice little fortune on this trip.

t_ch: 我怎么会出卖你呢？
t_en: How could I ever betray such a dear friend?

t_ch: 哎哟喂，这话说的，我都要感动哭了……
t_en: Uh-oh, someone's getting a little emotionally overinvested...

t_ch: …开什么玩笑，咱俩撑死了就是共犯关系，懂吗？共犯！谁想跟你有难同当啊喂！
t_en: You know what we are? Accomplices. You know what that means? Partners in crime! You know what else that means? We are not friends!

t_ch: 有福同享，有难…
t_en: Come hell or high water, we shall forge the bonds of brotherly—

t_ch: 喂喂喂，你脑子清醒点！咱俩撑死了就是共犯关系，懂吗？共犯！谁想跟你有难同当啊喂！
t_en: Whoa, whoa, whoa — Did you stop at a bar on the way over here? You know what we are? Accomplices. You know what that means? Partners in crime! You know what else that means? We're not friends!

t_ch: 行了行了，不扯没用的了。你赶紧跟我说说，上次那卷「微缩胶带」卖出多少价钱？
t_en: Alright, enough chinwagging. So, how much did the microfilm sell for?

t_ch: （糟了…只能随口编一个价钱了。希望不要太离谱……）
t_en: (Uh-oh. I'm just gonna have to make up an amount. Hope it's believable...)

t_ch: <unbreak>5000</unbreak>信用点…
t_en: <unbreak>5000</unbreak> credits...

t_ch: 啊？！你在逗我吗，五千？！
t_en: Huh? <unbreak>5000</unbreak>? You're kidding, right?

t_ch: 还是说，你…唔…这什么破宾馆啊，房门上怎么连个猫眼都没有？
t_en: How could it... Ugh, what kind of hotel is this, anyway? No peepholes on the doors?

t_ch: （他开始怀疑我了…这可不妙。）
t_en: (He's starting to suspect me... that's not good.)

t_ch: （下个问题无论如何都得考虑清楚再做回答。）
t_en: (I better think through the next question carefully before answering.)

t_ch: 小子，有件事我突然记不清了，你提醒我一下——
t_en: Y'know, I just realized there's something I forgot — maybe you could help me remember.

t_ch: ——之前我教你把「齿轮与智慧」拆散以后再藏起来，记得吧？你最后把它拆成了多少个部件来着？
t_en: That Gears and Wisdom sculpture I told you to take apart and hide away... Can you remind me how many pieces it had?

t_ch: 唔…好像是不到<unbreak>60</unbreak>个。
t_en: Uhh... a little less than <unbreak>60</unbreak>.

t_ch: …这才像话嘛！就知道你小子刚才又在跟我开玩笑，把我吓出一身冷汗。
t_en: Now that's more like it! You had me in a cold sweat with those jokes...

t_ch: 进来吧，赶紧进屋来，咱们好好讨论下接下来的生意怎么进行。
t_en: Here, hurry up and come inside. We need to discuss the details of our next operation.

t_ch: 你听到一阵清脆的「咔哒」声。寒腿叔叔打开了房门。
t_en: You hear a "click." Mr. Cold Feet opens the door.

t_ch: 轻轻地推门而入。
t_en: Gently push open the door and enter.

t_ch: 呃…大概<unbreak>40</unbreak>多个？
t_en: Uhh... about <unbreak>40</unbreak>?

t_ch: 很明显，你回答错了。寒腿叔叔这下不做声了…糟糕。
t_en: You obviously answered wrong. Not a peep can be heard from Mr. Cold Feet. Uh-oh....

t_ch: 你，解决了星核危机的英雄，大守护者敬重的战友宾朋…要是只因为眼前小小的一道宾馆房门就让犯人溜走了，岂不令人耻笑？
t_en: Wouldn't it be a shame if you, the hero of the Stellaron crisis, the supreme guardian's respected comrade-in-arms, let the prisoner slip away... just because of a hotel door?

t_ch: 留在你眼前的选择有，且只有一个……
t_en: There is only one choice left in front of you...

t_ch: 猛地一下破门而入！
